name: Ziai, Kamran

evidence: Rare surname; the only Kamran Ziai in Austin public records is the longtime UT Austin IT director who retired in 2023 after ~31 years at the university (LinkedIn/aggregator profiles list Austin, TX and Director of IT for Instructional Technologies and Design at Dell Medical School, plus earlier Director of IT at St. Edward's University). Filing occupation 'Retired IT' / employer 'Retired' matches exactly; central-Austin address (78703, with an older public listing at 78705) is consistent. No lobbyist registration, PAC ties, or advocacy-org roles surfaced.

name: Pena, Celina

name: Marsh, Lindsay

A 'Lindsay Marsh' appears in the Texas Tribune Government Salaries Explorer (employee record 1060772), which is consistent with the filing's employer 'State of texas', but that record page now 404s so the agency/title could not be verified. The two Austin-area LinkedIn profiles for the name work in consumer-brand and advertising roles, not state government, so neither corroborates. Industry recorded as Government solely on the self-reported employer field (categorize-by-employer fallback); the 'Performance' occupation string is too ambiguous to resolve. name: Patel, Ankur
resolved_employer: Self-employed physician — Ankur P. Patel, MD, adult cardiothoracic anesthesiologist practicing in Austin and Round Rock, TX
evidence: AMA Find-a-Doctor lists Ankur P Patel, MD, adult cardiothoracic anesthesiology, with a primary practice address at 901 W Ben White Blvd, Austin, TX 78704 — matching the filing's 78704 zip and 'Physician' occupation; 'Self' as employer is consistent with an independent/partnership anesthesia practice. Confidence held at medium because the name is common (a separate Ankur S Patel, DDS also practices in Austin) and the zip match may reflect the practice rather than a residence. No lobbyist registration, ideological-PAC record, or policy-advocacy board role surfaced.

name: Audelo, Sarah
resolved_employer: Open Society Foundations / Open Society Institute — Deputy Advocacy Director, States
evidence: Sarah Audelo is Deputy Advocacy Director, States at the Open Society Foundations (legal entity Open Society Institute), based in Washington, DC. FEC individual-contribution records show 110 contributions from AUDELO, SARAH at Washington DC zip 20020 listing employer OPEN SOCIETY INSTITUTE and occupation DIRECTOR / DEPUTY ADVOCACY DIRECTOR — an exact match to the D1 record's zip, occupation and employer strings. Previously Executive Director of the Alliance for Youth Action, Millennial/youth vote director for Hillary for America in 2016, and earlier a Special Projects Director at New Venture Fund.

affiliations.snippet: Audelo writes as an Open Society staff member about the foundation's support for state and local organizing; FEC filings list her employer as OPEN SOCIETY INSTITUTE with occupation DEPUTY ADVOCACY DIRECTOR.

Alliance for Youth Action announcing Sarah Audelo as its new Executive Director, noting her prior work as a teacher in Texas's Rio Grande Valley, a DC policy advocate, and youth vote director for Hillary Clinton's campaign.
FEC Schedule A record: AUDELO, SARAH, Washington DC 20020, OPEN SOCIETY INSTITUTE, DEPUTY ADVOCACY DIRECTOR — $250.00 to AQUI ESTAMOS PAC on 2024-04-14.

name: Suplina, Nicholas
resolved_employer: New York State (attorney); formerly Senior Vice President for Law and Policy, Everytown for Gun Safety
evidence: Confirmed as the gun-policy Nicholas (Nick) Suplina. FEC Schedule A records show NICHOLAS SUPLINA, Brooklyn NY 11215, occupation ATTORNEY, with employer listed as EVERYTOWN in 2024 and NY STATE in 2025-2026 — the same zip, occupation and employer string as the D1 record. He was Senior Vice President for Law and Policy at Everytown for Gun Safety and, before that, Senior Advisor and Special Counsel to the New York State Attorney General, where he led work on gun trafficking and ghost guns. The recipient, Amber Goodwin, is herself a national gun-violence-prevention organizer, which corroborates the identification. Note: his current employer string 'New York State' is what he self-reports on federal filings; his LinkedIn headline also lists an Executive Director role in firearms accountability, so the current title is not fully pinned down.

affiliations.role: Senior Vice President, Law and Policy (former)
affiliations.snippet: House Oversight Committee witness bio: 'Nick Suplina, Senior Vice President, Law and Policy, Everytown for Gun Safety.' Corroborated by FEC filings listing NICHOLAS SUPLINA, Brooklyn 11215, employer EVERYTOWN, occupation ATTORNEY.

affiliations.org: New York State Office of the Attorney General
affiliations.snippet: Bio: as Senior Advisor and Special Counsel to the NY State Attorney General he was lead author of the report 'Target on Trafficking', created a crime gun tracing database, and worked on combating untraceable ghost guns.

name: Moreno, Alfonso

evidence: Alfonso Moreno is Director of Digital Strategy at MooreCo Inc., a Temple, Texas designer and manufacturer of commercial and educational furniture, visual-communication and technology products; he is based in Austin and holds a BS in Mechanical Engineering from UT Austin. FEC records show MORENO, ALFONSO, Austin TX 78723, employer MOORECO INC., occupation MARKETING — an exact match to the D1 record on name, zip, employer and occupation. Industry mapped to Retail as the closest taxonomy label for a commercial-goods manufacturer/supplier (the taxonomy has no manufacturing category).

name: Scoor, Sabine
resolved_employer: 212 Catalysts — Founder & CEO (nonprofit consulting and fiscal-sponsorship intermediary, Austin area)
evidence: 212 Catalysts lists BiNi Scoor as Founder & CEO and Board Secretary; the firm is an Austin-area nonprofit consultancy and fiscal-sponsorship intermediary (EquiMediary) that has helped Black-led nonprofits secure over $26 million and supported roughly $600 million in funded projects across Texas. 'BiNi' appears to be the working name for the same person filing as Sabine Scoor — the D1 record's occupation 'CEO', employer '212 Catalysts', and Manor TX 78653 (greater Austin) all line up. Confidence held at medium because no public record directly ties the legal name 'Sabine' to 'BiNi'. No FEC contribution records exist under the Scoor surname.

affiliations.snippet: 212 Catalysts team page lists 'BiNi Scoor — Founder & CEO, Board Secretary'; the organization provides strategy, operations, financial and fiscal-sponsorship support to mission-driven nonprofits.
Austin Achieve Public Schools board roster lists 'BiNi Scoor – Chair'.
AALI Austin post titled '2023 Black X Conference: AALI co-founder, BiNi Scoor x KVUE'; 212 Catalysts' own team page also lists a 'Director of Administration - AALI' role, corroborating the tie.

The name is distinctive and the Austin location matches the 78747 filing address, though the filing itself lists no occupation or employer, so identity rests on name + city rather than a zip-level match.

name: Douglas, Anthony
resolved_employer: University of Chicago Medicine — general surgery resident physician (filing lists employer as 'Self'); founder of Lil' Scholars, a Chicago 501(c)(3)
evidence: Best match is Dr. Anthony Douglas II, MD, a University of Chicago general surgery resident living in the Hyde Park area (60615 adjoins the UChicago medical campus), which fits the filing's 'Doctor' occupation. Corroborating donation pattern: his signature policy work is gun violence prevention, and the recipient candidate, Amber Goodwin, is nationally known for founding a gun-violence-prevention organization for communities of color. 'Anthony Douglas' is a common name and the filing gives no employer, so confidence is held at medium rather than high.

He developed "a policy brief for the Responsibility in Firearms Legislation Alliance (RIFL)" proposing a no-fault victim compensation fund financed by the firearms industry.

The company installs structured data/voice cabling, security cameras and access control for commercial and residential customers across Central Texas. His Army service is personal military service, not a defense-contractor role, so no military_defense flag is warranted.

evidence: Luke Riley of Washington, DC is listed on LinkedIn and professional-data aggregators as a Business Intelligence Analyst at Navy Federal Credit Union — an exact match to the filed employer, occupation "Data Analyst," and DC location/zip 20018. His work history also shows prior roles with the United States Senate and Brightspeed, and a LaGrange College degree; SQL/Tableau/data-visualization skills are listed. Navy Federal Credit Union is a member-owned credit union serving military families, not a defense contractor, so no military_defense flag applies.
